title: Case 93892: College Decision 060/26/COL - Closure decision - Norway - Non-incorporation of Regulation 2023/444 on measures to ensure effective access to emergency services through emergency communications to the single European emergency number ‘112’
text:
Case No: 93892
Document No: 1593115
Decision No: 060/26/COL
EFTA SURVEILLANCE AUTHORITY DECISION
of 18 March 2026
closing Case No 93892 concerning the non-incorporation of
Regulation 2023/444 by Norway
Having regard to the Agreement between the EFTA States on the Establishment of a
Surveillance Authority and a Court of Justice, in particular Article 31 thereof.
On 15 May 2025, the EFTA Surveillance Authority issued a letter of formal notice to Norway
(Doc No 1531341), for its failure to incorporate the Act referred to at point 5czsd of Annex
XI to the EEA Agreement, Regulation 2023/444 on measures to ensure effective access to
emergency services through emergency communications to the single European
emergency number ‘112’ (“the Act”), with a compliance date of 1 February 2025. On 1
October 2025, the EFTA Surveillance Authority delivered a reasoned opinion to Norway
(Doc No 1561271).
By a Form 1 received on 27 February 2026 (Doc No 1593112), Norway informed the EFTA
Surveillance Authority that the Act had been made part of its internal legal order as of 1
March 2026.
It would appear that Norway has now taken the national measures necessary to ensure
that the Act has been made part of its internal legal order.
There are therefore no grounds for pursuing this matter further and the case is hereby
closed.
Done at Brussels,
For the EFTA Surveillance Authority,
For Arne Røksund Árni Páll Árnason Nuscha Wieczorek
President College Member Responsible College Member
College Member
For Melpo-Menie Joséphidès
Countersigning as Director,
Legal and Executive Affairs
This document has been electronically authenticated by Arni Pall Arnason, Sigrun
Ingibjorg Gisladottir.
Avenue des Arts 19H, B-1000 Brussels, tel: +32 2 286 18 11, www.eftasurv.int
